
Notes on how to update EDK2 to a newer version.
-----------------------------------------------

We generally pick a release branch to import from, rather than the master.
Like for instance UDK2010.SR0, UDK2014.SP1 or UDK2018.

The way we use "vendor branches" here is best described in the 1.7 subversion
book: http://svnbook.red-bean.com/en/1.7/svn.advanced.vendorbr.html (Version
1.8 of the book drops svn_load_dirs.pl in favor of 'svn checkout --force',
which doesn't properly deal with removed and renamed files.)

Because upstream is full of trailing whitespace, inconsistent line endings and
whatnot, we use a modified svn_load_dirs.pl script to clean these things up
as we do the importing of a new UDK release.

Steps (yyyy = previous version, xxxx = new version):

    0. Make sure vendor/edk2/current is correctly tagged, tag it if
       necessary (see step 5).  (We need the tag for the merge in step 7.)

    1. Get the upstream code from git:
           git clone https://github.com/tianocore/edk2.git git
           cd git
           git show-ref edk2-stable20xxxx  # (to get the commit hash for step 8)
           git archive --format=tar --output=../edk2-stable20xxxx.tar edk2-stable20xxxx
           mkdir ../edk2-stable20xxxx
           cd  ../edk2-stable20xxxx
           tar xf ../edk2-stable20xxxx.tar

    2. Check that all the new upstream files are covered by prop.tab, adjusting
       prop.tab if not:
           cd /fullpath/edk2-stable20xxxx
           find . -type f > /tmp/edk2-stable20xxxx-files
           grep -vE \
               "(`  sed -e '/^ *#/d' -e 's/[[:space:]]*break.*$//' -e '/^[[:space:]]*$/d' -e 's/$/|/' \
                        /fullpath/vendor/edk2/prop.tab \
                  | tr -d '\n'`nosuchextensionorfile)" \
               /tmp/edk2-stable20xxxx-files

    3. Now we need to set up a local subversion repository to do a dry-run of
       the importing.  This generally faster if adjustments to the prop.tab
       or svn_load_dirs.pl script are found to be needed.

       Create the repository:
           svnadmin create /tmp/dryrunrepo
           svn mkdir -m "3a" file:///tmp/dryrunrepo/vendor
           svn mkdir -m "3b" file:///tmp/dryrunrepo/vendor/edk2

       Import the current EDK2 vendor state (takes time):
           svn checkout file:///tmp/dryrunrepo/vendor/edk2 /tmp/dryrunwc
           svn copy https://linserv.de.oracle.com/vbox/svn/vendor/edk2/current /tmp/dryrunwc/current
           ln -sf /opt/X11/include /tmp/dryrunwc/current/EmulatorPkg/Unix/Host/X11IncludeHack # if commit fails
           svn ci -m "3c" /tmp/dryrunwc

       Tag the current EDK vendor state:
           svn copy -m "3d" file:///tmp/dryrunrepo/vendor/edk2/current file:///tmp/dryrunrepo/vendor/edk2/edk2-stable20yyyy

       Back up /tmp/dryrun* for quick restore.  This is optional.
           svn up /tmp/dryrunwc
           tar fcz ~/drynrunrestorepoint.tar.gz -C / tmp/dryrunwc tmp/dryrunrepo

    4. Import the new release into the vendor/edk2/current in the dryrunrepo
       using svn_load_dirs.pl (from the vbox repo):
           ./svn_load_dirs.pl -v -p prop.tab -fix_existing_properties -no_user_input -glob_ignores "" \
                file:///tmp/dryrunrepo \
                vendor/edk2/current \
                /fullpath/edk2-stable20xxxx/ \
                -m "Loading edk2-stable20xxxx (git commit xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx) bugref:4643" \
           |& tee edk2-imp-4.log

        Note! Make sure the svn path inside svn_load_dirs.pl matches the svn binary
              you are currently using.

        Grab a beverage of your choice, this will take some time.

    5. Check that you successfully imported everything:
           svn up /tmp/dryrunwc/current
           diff -rw /fullpath/edk2-stable20xxxx/ /tmp/dryrunwc/current

        Note! There are two dangling symlinks that diff may complain about.  Ignore.

    6. Do a dryrun merge:
           svn co \
               https://linserv.de.oracle.com/vbox/svn/trunk/src/VBox/Devices/EFI/Firmware \
               /tmp/dryrunmerge
           svn merge -v \
               file:///tmp/dryrunrepo/vendor/edk2/edk2-stable20yyyy \
               file:///tmp/dryrunrepo/vendor/edk2/current \
               /tmp/dryrunmerge

       Directories that we have deleted will end up as tree conflicts.  What
       you need to check for here are unnecessary conflicts due to incorrect
       svn properties, whitespace, eol or similar.

       If you need to make changes to prop.tab, svn_load_dirs.pl or use different
       options or something, do that and go back to step 3.  If you have a repo
       backup, you can restore this instead of doing step 3 over again.

    7. Commit changes to prop.tab, svn_load_dirs.pl and this document.

    8. Do the actual import into the vbox repository:
           ./svn_load_dirs.pl -v -p prop.tab -fix_existing_properties -no_user_input -glob_ignores "" \
                https://linserv.de.oracle.com/vbox/svn \
                vendor/edk2/current \
                /fullpath/edk2-stable20xxxx/ \
                -m "vendor/edk2: Loading edk2-stable20xxxx (git commit xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx). bugref:4643" \
           |& tee edk2-imp-8.log

    9. Update your vendor/edk2/current checkout and make sure everything looks okay.

   10. Tag current:
           svn copy current edk2-stable20xxxx
           svn ci -m "vendor/edk2: Tagged edk2-stable20xxxx. bugref:4643" edk2-stable20xxxx

   11. Go to trunk and make a copy of the current firmware:
           cd src/VBox/Devices/EFI
           svn copy Firmware FirmwareNew
           svn ci -m "efi: Preparing for updating to edk2-stable20xxxx.  bugref:4643"

   12. Do the dreaded threeway merge:
           cd FirmwareNew
           svn merge ^/vendor/edk2/edk2-stable20yyyy \
                     ^/vendor/edk2/current \
                     .

    13. Sometimes it might be necessary to update the included openssl version to match
        what EDK2 is expecting. Look for the required openssl version in the release notes
        of edk2-stable20xxxx. If an update is required check whether the version is
        in our vendor branch. If it isn't use the HowTo there to import it. When it is available
        do another threeway merge to update the openssl sources where y.y.yy is the old openssl
        and x.x.xx the new version:

           cd FirmwareNew
           svn merge ^/vendor/openssl/y.y.yy \
                     ^/vendor/openssl/x.x.xx \
                     CryptoPkg/Library/OpensslLib/openssl

    14. Sometimes it might be necessary to update the included pylibfdt version to match
        what EDK2 is expecting. Look for the required pylibfdt version in the release notes
        of edk2-stable20xxxx. If an update is required check whether the version is
        in our vendor branch. If it isn't use the HowTo there to import it. When it is available
        do another threeway merge to update the pylibfdt sources where y.y.yy is the old pylibfdt
        and x.x.xx the new version:

           cd FirmwareNew
           svn merge ^/vendor/pylibfdt/y.y.yy \
                     ^/vendor/pylibfdt/x.x.xx \
                     MdePkg/Library/BaseFdtLib/libfdt

   15. Resolve all conflicts, make it build and commit.

   16. Temporarily modify src/VBox/Devices/Makefile.kmk to pick up the
       EFI/FirmwareNew/Makefile.kmk instead of EFI/Firmware/Makefile.kmk so
       you can get some real testing while having a fallback (EFI/Firmware).

   17. Once you've tested it and believe is is usable, revert the Maekfile
       changes from the previous step, remove the previous Firmware directory
       and rename the new firmware directory:
           cd src/VBox/Devices
           ${EDITOR} Makefile.kmk
           svn delete EFI/Firmware
           svn ren EFI/FirmwareNew EFI/Firmware
           svn ci -m "efi: Removed old EFI firmware.  Using edk2-stable20xxxx from now on. bugref:4643"

   18. Did you postpone step 10?  Do it now.
